title: Vail PD - Data Transfer to LexisNexis

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request is related to your agency's use of LexisNexis Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC), and Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX). 

We are seeking
1) Technical Specifications: Any Interface Control Document (ICD) or technical implementation guide that defines the data structure and data elements for the automated export of Records Management System (RMS) or Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) data to the "CISC Data Warehouse" or the LexisNexis "PSDEX private container".
2) Data Mapping: Any "Data Translation" guides, field-mapping tables, or schemas used to configure the transfer of local agency data into the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Virtual Crime Center (VCC / AVCC) platforms.
3) Field Lists: Any record or "Schedule A" line item defining the "Database Interface" capabilities, specifically identifying which categories of information (e.g., Victim PII, Witness Contact Data, Real-Time Jail Bookings) are included in the nightly (or regular) automated feed.

Exclusion Note: We are not seeking URLs, passwords, network IP addresses, encryption keys, or sensitive security information, or contents of any data fields. We are seeking only the field name of the data elements (e.g., "Name," "DOB," "SSN," "Narrative") that have been approved for mass transfer to LexisNexis/PSDEX. These files likely sit within your agency's IT department, or the individual(s) responsible for the AVCC integration.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: Jefferson County (CO) Sheriffs Office (CISC Member) - Accurint/LexisNexis Data Sharing

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

In regards to your agency's LexisNexis/Accurint Data sharing scope - We are seeking to understand:
1) Does your agency contribute data to LexisNexis/Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC)/PSDEX?
2) If so, do you limit which LexisNexis AVCC customers your data is shared with?
3) If you do limit data sharing by agency, what agencies have access to JCSO data via AVCC? 

Specifically, we are asking
Records that show whether JCSO has authorized its contributed law enforcement data to be shared with agencies or entities outside the Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) through the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC).
- If JCSO has NOT authorized sharing beyond the CISC, please provide a screenshot, system export, or other record from the AVCC or LexisNexis administrative interface confirming that JCSO data distribution is limited to the CISC private container.
- If JCSO HAS authorized sharing beyond the CISC, please provide a screenshot, system export, or other record identifying the agencies or entity categories with whom JCSO's data is shared.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: Aurora PD - LexisNexis Accurint Data Sharing Details

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Aurora PD - LexisNexis Accurint Data Sharing Details
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Request 1 — Data Sharing Scope (Outside CISC):

Records that show whether Aurora PD has authorized its contributed law enforcement data to be shared with agencies or entities outside the Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) through the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC).
- If Aurora PD has NOT authorized sharing beyond the CISC, please provide a screenshot, system export, or other record from the AVCC or LexisNexis administrative interface confirming that Aurora PD's data distribution is limited to the CISC private container.
- If Aurora PD HAS authorized sharing beyond the CISC, please provide a screenshot, system export, or other record identifying the agencies or entity categories with whom Aurora PD's data is shared.
---
Request 2 — Query Audit Files (January 1, 2025 – April 30, 2026)

All records showing which agencies, entities, or user accounts queried AVCC (or other LexisNexis Risk Solutions products) for data contributed by the Aurora Police Department during the period January 1, 2026 through April 1, 2026.
- Note: I am requesting query metadata only — specifically: the name or identifier of the querying agency or user account, the date of the query, and the platform used. I am not requesting the content of any search results, case information, or personally identifiable information about any individual within records belonging to Aurora PD or LexisNexis.
- Section 4(vi) of the LexisNexis Master Terms & Conditions – CISC states that LexisNexis "shall reasonably assist Customers in conducting audits of their use of the LN Services and in conducting internal investigations." Aurora PD has a contractual right to request these records from LexisNexis.
 - If Aurora PD has previously requested audit records from LexisNexis covering any portion of the period January 1, 2025 through April 30, 2026, please provide all files Aurora PD received or exported from LexisNexis in response to that request.
 - If Aurora PD has not previously requested an audit of who has accessed the agency's contributed data within AVCC or PSDEX, please provide written confirmation that no such audit has been conducted and that no responsive records exist.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: LAPR - LexisNexis Sharing of CopLogic/BuyCrash Data

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Context: LAPD's website links to LexisNexis' BuyCrash platform to purchase LA-area crash reports. We would like to understand if LAPD vehicle crash data is shared with other LexisNexis systems (e.g. PSDEX), and if so, what data is shared? 

To help answer this question, the following records are requested: 
1. The current contract between LAPD and LexisNexis Risk Solutions (or any subsidiary - including BuyCrash/CopLogic) for the online distribution of traffic collision reports — including all exhibits, schedules, and amendments.
2. Any addenda (with LexisNexis or BuyCrash/Coplogic) or amendments to that contract governing data sharing, data contribution, or data licensing — specifically any document referencing "PSDEX," "Public Safety Data Exchange," "Accurint Virtual Crime Center," "AVCC," "data contribution," or "XML Addendum."
3. Any agreement, MOU, or data sharing arrangement identifying which third parties — including other agencies, law enforcement consortiums, federal entities, or commercial subscribers — may access or receive LAPD traffic collision report data submitted through the BuyCrash/Coplogic platform.
4. Any privacy impact assessment, data governance review, or legal opinion prepared by or for LAPD regarding the downstream use of LAPD crash data by LexisNexis or its affiliates.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

a. directed to private counsel, public defenders, prosecuting attorneys, guardians ad litem, and court-appointed special advocates;

1.  Microsoft Purview unified audit log entries for the listed accounts, exported in their native CSV or JSON format from the Microsoft Purview compliance portal (compliance.microsoft.com), without summarization or filtering. c. File activity (SharePoint and OneDrive):

All files and folders accessed, downloaded, modified, uploaded, renamed, moved, or copied
All files and folders deleted, including from the first-stage recycle bin and the second-stage recycle bin
All deletions of file version history
All changes to file or folder sharing, including the creation or removal of sharing links, anonymous links, and access permissions

d. Compliance, retention, and eDiscovery activity:

Any content search created, started, or exported through the Microsoft Purview compliance portal
Any eDiscovery case opened
Any litigation hold or retention hold created, modified, or removed for the account or its associated mailbox or storage
Any retention policy created, modified, or removed that affects the account

All sign-in events, successful and failed, including timestamp, source IP address, and device used
Any password resets

Any changes to user account settings or group memberships

6. Mailbox and OneDrive retention hold status records for the listed accounts, including any litigation hold, retention policy, or in-place hold applied to or removed from those accounts at any time in 2026.

communications.subject: FW: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: RFP List
communications.communication:
Good afternoon:

This is in response to your email correspondence below. Please be advised that the City construes your request for records as a request coming under New Hampshire’s Right-to-Know law, RSA chapter 91-A.
Please be further advised that the City has no obligation to respond to requests from out-of-state corporations unless the corporation can show that it is located and/or has a nexus in New Hampshire. See McBurney v. Young, 569 U.S. 221 (2013). The City receives a large number of requests from out-of-state data collection corporations requesting financial and other reports to be submitted electronically to an email address and/or website. Due to the significant amount of time it takes for staff to research and generate reports in response to the out-of-state requests from data collection companies, the City will not transmit responses to such requests.

Please note that the City is required to make documents which are subject to disclosure, accessible for public review on its regular business premises (RSA Chapter 91-A:4,I). In accordance with RSA 91-A, the City would provide you, or a Representative (Mr. Van Fleet), the opportunity to personally review records, free of charge, at City Hall.  To the extent that your Representative would request copies of any documents in electronic format, your Representative may bring a sealed/unopened thumb drive and the City will upload the documents to the thumb drive, free of charge. If your Representative does not have a sealed/unopened thumb drive at the time of the scheduled visit to City Hall, we have new thumb drives that may be purchased at cost price.

Please contact our office at 603-225-8505 to schedule a date and time for you to review the documents at City Hall. The City will require approximately thirty (30) days prior notice of the date of when you intend to review the documents so that we can have the documents ready for your review upon your arrival.

title: Washington County Public Schools Open Records Request - Student enrollment data

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Washington County Public Schools Open Records Request - Student enrollment data
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records Coordinator,

I am writing on behalf of the Data Acquisition Lab to formally request access to public records under the Maryland Public Information Act, Title 4. We are requesting aggregated counts of student enrollment at the most granular geographic level available, which will enable a more meaningful understanding of community-level trends. We plan to request this information on a bi-annual basis going forward.

I am specifically requesting a tabular dataset containing the counts of enrolled students in the Washington County Public Schools for the current (2025-2026) school year, disaggregated by the following fields:
- School name
- United States Census Tract
- Grade level (Pre-K through 12, or the finest granularity available for each school)

Please provide this data in electronic format (CSV preferred), including the following fields where available:
- School identifier (name, NCES ID)
- Census Tract GEOID (full 11-digit or abbreviated 6-digit code)
- Grade level
- Student enrollment count

Privacy Considerations:
- This request does not seek, nor does it require, personally identifiable information (PII) or any data points that could reasonably be used to identify individual students or households.
- We respect the importance of compliance with all applicable privacy laws. If additional thresholds or adjustments are required to ensure absolute privacy, we are happy to collaborate on refinements to the data as necessary.

If certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation of the specific exemption(s) and release any non-exempt portions of the requested records. If this data is not centrally held, please advise which agency holds this data. Thank you for your attention to this request. If you have any questions or require further information, please feel free to contact me at request@applieddataproject.org.

title: AI chat logs (Town Of Durham)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the last 30 days of ChatGPT, Anthropic Claude, or Google Gemini chat logs of your agency’s executive leadership as well as the chat logs of all of their direct reports and the respective AI chat programs.

ChatGPT logs can be accessed through the following steps:

1. **Click on your name or profile icon** (bottom-left corner of the ChatGPT interface).

2. Select **"Settings"**.

3. Go to the **"Data Controls"** tab.

4. Click **"Export data"**.

5. A pop-up will appear — click **"Confirm export"**.

6. OpenAI will email you a download link with a `.zip` file containing your chat history in JSON format (and HTML for easy viewing).

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records related to the Department’s Flock Safety system:

1. All active contracts, agreements, amendments, memoranda of understanding, statements of work, purchase orders, and any associated privacy addenda, data sharing agreements, or terms of service incorporated by reference between the Department and Flock Safety, from January 1, 2017 to the date this request is processed.

2. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2017, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

3. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2017, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

4. A list of network share settings that include:
4a. Flock networks shared with me
4b. Networks that I’m sharing
4c. Date(s) when share access was granted for these networks and date of revocation of access
4d. Date(s) when the statewide lookup feature was enabled and date(s) it was disabled
4e. Date(s) when the nationwide lookup feature was enabled and date(s) it was disabled

This information may be available in the Flock Network Sharing Tool.

"The audit logs can be accessed via your agency's Flock admin dashboard on the "Insights" tab. These logs may show the date/time of search, user ID, and search parameters and reason. Customers may download these logs in the user interface as spreadsheets in 31-day increments. It is recommended that Flock customer support assist customers with the downloads when feasible for timeframes larger than six months and major accounts."

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Schedule E rental real estate examination statistics, tax years 2019-2025
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide, for tax years 2019 - 2025 (or the most recent year for which the IRS has data), the number of individual tax returns with Schedule E rental real estate activity that were selected for examination, broken down by AGI band, with examination outcomes (no change, additional tax assessed) and average additional tax assessed per examination. 

For years where examinations are still in progress, please provide a count of audits opened to date with a note indicating the year is still active. 

I confirm I have reviewed IRS Publication 1415, Publication 5869, the IRS Data Book audit coverage tables, and GAO-24-106449, and am seeking the Schedule E-specific breakdown not contained in those publications.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.
